meta.setting: Pine camps, creek bottoms, berry brush, store lots, and livestock edges around French Gulch
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western with hunting, camp survival, animal pressure, scarcity, and hard choices

research_grounding.title: California Department of Fish and Wildlife Black Bear
research_grounding.use: Black bear habitat, omnivorous food behavior, forest/chaparral range, and human conflict around food, garbage, and livestock.

research_grounding.title: NPS The Wintu
research_grounding.use: Clear Creek food systems and land knowledge context, handled respectfully without invented ceremonial content.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the Bear Sign cast page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet. This JSON contains shape and guidance only.
page_shape: square image reference immediately after the title
opening blockquote with invented camp warning, flour-sack note, or track saying generated later
H2 archetype subtitle in the register of Black Bear Pressure
one or two compact lore paragraphs introducing black bear presence as trail risk, food thief, livestock trouble, and force of the local hills
H3 Role with a short table-facing function phrase
H3 Traits with two to four grounded signs or behaviors
10 H4 headings, each followed later by a brief playable move, warning, cost, edge, or complication
H3 Camp Say closing blockquote with plain meaning generated later
generation_guidance: Generate black bears as real animals: omnivores, climbers, food-driven, strong, expressive, and dangerous when cornered or trained by bad camp habits.
Tie future entries to tracks, raided camp stores, livestock conflict, cub caution, bluff charge, honey, garbage, carcasses, berry patches, and winter den mistakes.
Do not make bears monsters, villains, or spiritual mascots; make human negligence and scarcity matter.
Make the page useful for hunting restraint, camp routines, food storage, tracking, and tense non-combat choices.
Do not include final move names or final move text in this JSON.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: The bear should feel less like an enemy than a bill for sloppy living. A camp that feeds a bear has already made its first bad decision.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A bear-sign scene should ask who left food out, what the animal learned, and whether the riders can solve the problem without making it worse.
must_preserve: Use 1899 western animal-sign and camp language only.
Keep bears naturalistic, dangerous, and ecologically grounded.
The generated page must include image path cast/images/bear-sign.jpg.
No finished page prose, final entries, or final sayings belong in this prompt file.
avoid: do not mention any prior packet, inherited taxonomy, or non-western source language
do not include medieval, enchanted, sacred-invented, or fake-tribal framing
do not make the bear a monster, pet, boss fight, mascot, or moral symbol
do not use modern wildlife gear, vehicles, plastic coolers, or clean theme-park staging
self_check: The future markdown follows the exact heading sequence.
The future page is about real bear sign, camp consequences, food pressure, and table action.
The prompt contains no finished page prose.
All language belongs to grounded 1899 Jefferson State western play.
